device_name: EPIQ Series Diagnostic Ultrasound System; Affiniti Series Diagnostic Ultrasound System
intended_use: The R-Trigger algorithm software feature on Philips EPIQ and Affiniti Ultrasound System is intended to support detection of R-wave peak (R-trigger) as an input to certain TTE clinical applications, in
intended_use_full: The R-Trigger algorithm software feature on Philips EPIQ and Affiniti Ultrasound System is intended to support detection of R-wave peak (R-trigger) as an input to certain TTE clinical applications, initially including AutoStrain LV, AutoEF, 2D Auto LV (collectively referred to as "AutoStrain"), and AutoMeasure applications.
target_condition: Cardiac cycle analysis for transthoracic echocardiography; enables automated cardiac measurements (ejection fraction, global longitudinal strain, ventricular dimensions, Doppler parameters) when ECG signal is not available
ground_truth: ECG-based R-trigger detection from on-cart physio board (reference standard)
other_metrics: R-wave peak time stamp: Lower LoA -58.06ms (95% CI: -59.34, -56.78), Upper LoA 69.69ms (95% CI: 68.41, 70.97). AutoStrain EF: Lower LoA -6.64% (95% CI: -7.60%, -5.68%), Upper LoA 6.52% (95% CI: 5.56%, 7.49%). AutoStrain GLS: Lower LoA -5.25% (95% CI: -5.72%, -4.79%), Upper LoA 4.63% (95% CI: 4.16%, 5.10%). Pearson correlation for EF: 0.892 (95% CI: 0.853, 0.922). Pearson correlation for GLS: 0.992 (95% CI: 0.990, 0.994). AutoMeasure tested on 35 hypotheses with Bland-Altman limits of agreement reported for 32 cardiac measurement parameters including velocities, distances, volumes, and time intervals.
